root_motivation: Engineering and Patrick need a single machine-readable + human-readable document that maps every AC from the four grooming spikes (ATSCALE-49212-49215) to a concrete produced artifact (or an honest gap/skip verdict), so no one mistakes 'we measured the token side' for 'we closed the spike' and the hand-back brief is checksum-verifiable against the fleet's actual outputs.
user_persona: Joe Yen (spike owner) runs this once after the four sibling CLIs ship; output goes to the grooming thread and to Patrick as the spike-closure evidence package.

acceptance_criteria.description: Given all four artifact inputs + a ticket-map, the bundle emits spike_evidence.json with one entry per ticket and a per-AC verdict for every AC in the map.

An AC whose expected artifact field is present with a real value is verdicted 'produced' with a value_summary echoing the value; an AC whose artifact is absent is 'skip-needs-live' or 'gap' (never 'produced').
A 'skip-needs-live' verdict carries a non-empty blocked_on naming the unmet dependency; the run still exits 0.
The per-ticket summary counts sum to the number of ACs in the map for that ticket (every AC gets exactly one verdict — no AC dropped, no double-count).
--format markdown emits one section per ticket with an AC→verdict→evidence table, and skip-needs-live rows render their blocked_on.
Malformed JSON in any input exits 2 with a stderr diagnostic naming the bad file, never a panic; a missing optional artifact degrades its ACs to skip-needs-live, not a crash.
cargo test passes offline on fixtures; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_AC7.rs — clippy_warning_count == 0 AND all prior ACs green

scope: Reads four artifact JSON files (footprint, paramq bench report, walkthrough transcript, handle-demo) plus a ticket-map JSON
Maps each ticket AC in the ticket-map to a verdict: produced | gap | skip-needs-live
Emits spike_evidence.json with per-AC verdicts and per-ticket summary counts
Emits --format markdown brief with AC→verdict→evidence tables per ticket
Exits 0 on honest gaps/skips, exits 2 on malformed input
non_goals: Producing any measurement itself — it only reads the sibling outputs
Deciding whether a spike should ship — it organizes evidence; engineering decides
Inventing AC coverage that artifacts don't support — missing value is gap, never produced
Network access or live DAX/MDX port execution
Integrating or calling the sibling CLIs directly — it reads their output JSON only

additional.allowed_deps: serde, serde_json, clap — no network, no measurement logic

five_whys_trace.q: Why build a capstone bundler when the four CLIs already emit JSON?
five_whys_trace.a: Because four separate JSON files scattered across four repos is not a finding; engineering needs 'here is AC1, here is the number, here is what's still a gap.'

five_whys_trace.q: Why does engineering need an AC→verdict mapping rather than just reading the raw JSON?
five_whys_trace.a: Because each spike's ACs are only partially covered by the fleet — the honest gaps (live LLM vendor, live DAX port, SE-DEMO access) must be named explicitly so no one mistakes partial coverage for full spike closure.

five_whys_trace.q: Why must the gaps be named in a machine-readable artifact rather than prose?
five_whys_trace.a: So the grooming thread can verify the hand-back brief against the ticket's ACs by checksum rather than by reading prose — the spike convention says the deliverable is documented evidence, not a narrative.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What is the exact shape of the ticket-map input?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: A checked-in JSON file: {tickets: [{ticket, acs: [{id, text, expected_artifact, owner, verdict_if_absent?}]}]}. The bundle scores each AC against the named artifact file.

ambiguities_resolved.question: How does the bundle decide 'gap' vs 'skip-needs-live' for absent artifacts?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: The ticket-map AC entry carries an optional 'verdict_if_absent' field; if set to 'skip-needs-live' it must also carry 'blocked_on'. If absent, the bundle defaults to 'gap'.
